title: gaultheria paucinervia
intro: Gaultheria paucinervia, also known as Eastern teaberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to eastern North America. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Gaultheria paucinervia include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and support respiratory health. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with its leaves and stems being used in herbal preparations to promote overall well-being.
